title: Jordan Kahu

infobox.value: (1991-01-28) 28 January 1991 (age 35)  [Lower Hutt](/wiki/Lower_Hutt "Lower Hutt"), Wellington, New Zealand

(born 28 January 1991) is a former professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who played as a [winger](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Wing "Rugby league positions") or [centre](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Centre "Rugby league positions"), [fullback](/wiki/Fullback_(rugby_league) "Fullback (rugby league)") for the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") at international level.

**Jordan Kahu** (born 28 January 1991) is a former professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who played as a [winger](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Wing "Rugby league positions") or [centre](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Centre "Rugby league positions"), [fullback](/wiki/Fullback_(rugby_league) "Fullback (rugby league)") for the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") at international level.

Kahu has played for the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") and the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"), as well as the [World All Stars](/wiki/NRL_All_Stars_team "NRL All Stars team") and [New Zealand Māori](/wiki/New_Zealand_M%C4%81ori_rugby_league_team "New Zealand Māori rugby league team") in the "All Star" representative stage.[[1]](#cite_note-Statistics_at_rugbyleagueproject.org-1)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Born in [Lower Hutt](/wiki/Lower_Hutt "Lower Hutt"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"), Kahu is of [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_people "Māori people") descent specifically the [Ngāti Toa](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Toa "Ngāti Toa") [Iwi](/wiki/Iwi "Iwi") (tribe). Kahu is short for Jordan's birth surname Kahukuranui. Kahu attended Wellington College and played his junior [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") for the [Petone Rugby Club](/wiki/Petone_Rugby_Club "Petone Rugby Club") with [Lima Sopoaga](/wiki/Lima_Sopoaga "Lima Sopoaga") before moving to Australia to attend [Keebra Park State High School](/wiki/Keebra_Park_State_High_School "Keebra Park State High School"). While at Keebra Park, Kahu captained the school's [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") side to win the 2009 [Arrive Alive Cup](/wiki/Arrive_Alive_Cup "Arrive Alive Cup"). Kahu played for the Broncos [NYC](/wiki/National_Youth_Competition_(rugby_league) "National Youth Competition (rugby league)") team in 2010 and 2011, scoring 11 tries and kicking 2 goals in 23 games.[[2]](#cite_note-Statistics_at_rleague.com-2) Kahu's 2011 and 2012 seasons were shattered by two season ending knee injuries.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Playing career")]

### 2013

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2013")]

In Round 4 of the [2013 NRL season](/wiki/2013_NRL_season "2013 NRL season"), Kahu made his [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") debut for the Brisbane Broncos against the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") at centre in the 32–26 loss at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) In the Round 9 match against the [Parramatta Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels"), Kahu scored his first and second NRL tries in the Broncos 19–18 loss at [Parramatta Stadium](/wiki/Parramatta_Stadium "Parramatta Stadium").[[6]](#cite_note-6) Soon after, Kahu extended his contract with the Broncos for 2 years, keeping him at the club until the end of the 2015 season.[[7]](#cite_note-7) He scored 4 tries from 10 appearances in his debut year.

### 2014

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2014")]

Kahu injured his knee again for a third time in four years in a trial match against the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys").[[8]](#cite_note-8) In Round 19 against the [New Zealand Warriors](/wiki/New_Zealand_Warriors "New Zealand Warriors"), Kahu played his first match for the Broncos of the 2014 season playing on the wing for the suspended [Daniel Vidot](/wiki/Daniel_Vidot "Daniel Vidot") and scoring a try in the 28–22 win.[[9]](#cite_note-9) He finished off the [season](/wiki/2014_NRL_season "2014 NRL season") playing in 3 matches and scoring three tries.

### 2015

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2015")]

Due to the injury of incumbent fullback [Darius Boyd](/wiki/Darius_Boyd "Darius Boyd") during the off-season, Kahu was selected to fill in at [fullback](/wiki/Fullback_(rugby_league) "Fullback (rugby league)") showing promising performances in the early rounds.[[10]](#cite_note-10) During a "breakout season", Kahu was rewarded with a 2-year contract extension with Broncos, to the end of the 2017 season.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) On 4 October, Kahu played on the wing in the Broncos' 17-16 golden point extra time loss to the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") in the [2015 NRL Grand Final](/wiki/2015_NRL_Grand_Final "2015 NRL Grand Final").[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) He finished off the [2015 season](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season") having played in 22 matches, scoring 9 tries and kicking 22 goals.[[15]](#cite_note-15) On 9 October, he was selected in the 23-man [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") squad to tour England. Kahu was one of four members of Brisbane's Grand Final team to be included in coach Stephen Kearney's squad for the [Three-Test tour](/wiki/2015_New_Zealand_rugby_league_tour_of_Great_Britain "2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain") and one of six rookies to have received an international call-up by the Kiwis.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) On 2 November, he made his New Zealand international debut against [England](/wiki/England_national_rugby_league_team "England national rugby league team"), playing at centre in the Kiwis' 26–12 loss at [KC Stadium](/wiki/KC_Stadium "KC Stadium").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/Jordan_Kahu.jpg/250px-Jordan_Kahu.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jordan_Kahu.jpg)

Kahu representing the Kiwis

On 14 November, in the third and final test match against [England](/wiki/England_national_rugby_league_team "England national rugby league team"), he scored his first international try for New Zealand in the Kiwis' 20–14 loss at [DW Stadium](/wiki/DW_Stadium "DW Stadium").[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### 2016

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2016")]

On 13 February, Kahu played for the [World All Stars](/wiki/NRL_All_Stars_team "NRL All Stars team") against the [Indigenous All Stars](/wiki/Indigenous_All_Stars_(rugby_league) "Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)"), playing at fullback in his team's 12–8 win at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Lang_Park "Lang Park").[[20]](#cite_note-20) In the Round 1 season opening match against the [Parramatta Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels"), he scored the first try of the [2016 season](/wiki/2016_NRL_season "2016 NRL season") in the Broncos' 17–4 win at [Parramatta Stadium](/wiki/Parramatta_Stadium "Parramatta Stadium").[[21]](#cite_note-21) On 6 May, Kahu played for New Zealand against [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team") in the [2016 Anzac Test](/wiki/2016_Anzac_Test "2016 Anzac Test"), playing at fullback in the 16–0 loss at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Hunter_Stadium "Hunter Stadium").[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) Kahu finished the [2016 NRL season](/wiki/2016_NRL_season "2016 NRL season") with him playing in 20 matches, scoring 15 tries and kicking 42 goals for the Broncos. On 21 September 2016, Kahu was added to the [New Zealand national rugby league team](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") train-on squad for the [2016 Four Nations](/wiki/2016_Rugby_League_Four_Nations "2016 Rugby League Four Nations").[[24]](#cite_note-24) On 3 October 2016, Kahu was selected in the final 24-man squad.[[25]](#cite_note-25) Kahu played in 4 matches of tournament including starting at fullback and scoring 2 tries in the Kiwis 34-8 Four Nations Final loss against Australia at [Anfield](/wiki/Anfield "Anfield").[[26]](#cite_note-26)

### 2017

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2017")]

In February 2017, Kahu was selected in the Broncos [2017 NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/2017_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2017 NRL Auckland Nines") squad.[[27]](#cite_note-27) In Round 1 season opening match against the [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks"), Kahu put his name in the record books by scoring the first try of the 2017 season in the Broncos 26–18 win at [Shark Park](/wiki/Shark_Park "Shark Park"), Kahu is the first player to score the first try of the season in two consecutive seasons. On 5 May 2017, Kahu played for New Zealand in the [2017 ANZAC Test](/wiki/2017_ANZAC_Test "2017 ANZAC Test") against Australia where he started at centre and kicked 2 goals in the 30–12 loss at [Canberra Stadium](/wiki/Canberra_Stadium "Canberra Stadium").[[28]](#cite_note-28) On 5 August 2017, Kahu extended his contract with the Broncos to the end of the 2020 season.[[29]](#cite_note-29) Kahu finished the [2017 NRL season](/wiki/2017_NRL_season "2017 NRL season") with him playing in 25 matches, scoring 9 tries and kicking 74 goals and 3 field goals for the Broncos. Kahu was in the frame to be selected in the Kiwis [2017 Rugby League World Cup](/wiki/2017_Rugby_League_World_Cup "2017 Rugby League World Cup") squad but opted to sit out the tournament to recover from a groin injury.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

### 2018

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2018")]

In Round 2 of the [2018 NRL season](/wiki/2018_NRL_season "2018 NRL season"), Kahu fractured his jaw in the Broncos' win over the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys").[[31]](#cite_note-31) He returned 10 weeks later in the Broncos' Round 12 win over the [Parramatta Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels").[[32]](#cite_note-32) He finished the 2018 season with 15 games, two tries, seven goals and 22 points, his lowest tally in all categories since 2015.

### 2019

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2019")]

On 15 February, Kahu represented the [Māori All Stars](/wiki/New_Zealand_M%C4%81ori_rugby_league_team "New Zealand Māori rugby league team") side, kicking a goal in the 14–34 loss to the [Indigenous All Stars](/wiki/Indigenous_All_Stars_(rugby_league) "Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)").[[33]](#cite_note-33) On 18 February, Kahu joined the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") on a one-year deal.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

In Round 1 of the [2019 NRL season](/wiki/2019_NRL_season "2019 NRL season"), he made his debut for the Cowboys, starting at fullback in their 24–12 win over the [St George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons").[[35]](#cite_note-35) In Round 17, he fractured his eye socket in the Cowboys' 15–12 win over the [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters"), missing five weeks.[[36]](#cite_note-36) He returned in Round 22, playing the final four games of the season.[[37]](#cite_note-37) He ended the 2019 season as the Cowboys' top point scorer, with 86 points in 19 games.

On 13 September, it was announced that he would be leaving the Cowboys at the end of the season.[[38]](#cite_note-38) With clauses in his previous contract with the Broncos still standing, Kahu returned to the Broncos for the 2019-2020 pre-season.

### 2020

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2020")]

Hampered by the injuries and indifferent form of their available centres and wingers, Kahu made only two appearances for Brisbane in the [2020 NRL season](/wiki/2020_NRL_season "2020 NRL season"), a year in which the club finished last on the table for the first time in their history.[[39]](#cite_note-39)

## 2021

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jordan_Kahu&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2021")]

Kahu announced that he would retire at the end of the [2021 NRL season](/wiki/2021_NRL_season "2021 NRL season").[[40]](#cite_note-40)
